items.title: Burlington encourages residents to prepare for hot weather
items.content_text: Burlington officials are advising residents to prepare for hot weather, noting that vulnerable populations face higher risks of heat-related illness. Why included: Public health advisory regarding vulnerable populations.
items.snippet: ... fully adjusted to warmer weather, heat illnesses are more common. Older adults, young children, people who are pregnant, outdoor workers, and those with chronic health conditions, mental health conditions, disabilities, or limited access to cooling are at increased risk of heat-related illness. People experiencing homelessness or living without air conditioning face particularly elevated risks from prolonged heat exposure and dehydratio ...
items.summary: Burlington officials are advising residents to prepare for hot weather, noting that vulnerable populations face higher risks of heat-related illness.
items.reason: Public health advisory regarding vulnerable populations.

items.title: Vermont’s largest health insurer wants to offer a cheaper plan. Can it actually help Vermonters?
items.content_text: BCBSVT is seeking to offer more affordable health insurance plans to address declining enrollment in the state's marketplace. Why included: Directly concerns BCBSVT strategy and public affordability.
tags: Blue Cross and Blue Shield of Vermont
matchedTerms: Blue Cross and Blue Shield of Vermont
items.snippet: ...  of Vermonters receiving that assistance dropped by more than 9,000. The number of people buying any insurance at all on Vermont’s marketplace fell by more than 2,000, too. Since, BlueCross BlueShield of Vermont has looked for ways to offer less expensive plans. “We went hunting,” Andrew Garland, BCBS’s vice president of client relations and external affairs, told the Green Mountain Care Board in a May 6 hearing outlining a new suite of ...

As of late 2025, that dynamic has dissolved into a public confrontation that places Vermont patient ...

items.content_text: VTDigger's Post VTDigger 2h · BlueCross BlueShield wants to offer a new suite of cheaper plans, but the state’s primary healthcare regulator is casting doubt on whether that would actually yield any real savings.vtdigger.orgVermont’s largest health insurer wants to offer a cheaper plan. Can it actually help Vermonters? - VTDiggerAll reactions:814 comments2 sharesLikeCommentMost ... Comments: Janet Fitzpatrick: Allow people over 30 to buy Blue Cross Blue Shield catastrophic plans! Those plans are much more affordable. | Mark Byland: sp/healthcare/healthprofit | Kristin Bryn Reid: The health insurance system in Vermont is voing exactly as designed for the last 20 or so years We tried to warn you | Dylan Greer: Or hear me out… we pay a boat load of money to insurance companies… they can pay for our healthcare | Janice Wright: Cheaper plan = lower monthly payments, but higher deductibles, higher co-pays, less coverage, and fewer in-network doctors | Jay Files: My work just switched to MVP gold great insurance we had blue cross it covers almost nothing worthless insurance you have to fight with them for everything | Amy Beth: "higher" deductible plan my deductible is already $9500 so unless they are significantly dropping the monthly premium which is between $809-900 a month for one person this isn't helpful. Health insurance for those of us who have to purchase it from the marketplace is ridiculous. | Ruth Miller: I dunno...I just got a class action suit settlement payment from BCBS. I'm done with them, especially after leaving me with no gap coverage after they pulled Blue Advantage out of VT. They seem to lose money on a continuing basis. | Matthew Funk: It’s almost like y’all already Fcked it up
items.snippet: VTDigger's Post VTDigger 2h · BlueCross BlueShield wants to offer a new suite of cheaper plans, but the state’s primary healthcare regulator is casting doubt on whether that would actually yield any real savings.vtdigger.orgVermont’s largest health insurer wants to offer a cheaper plan. Can it actually help Vermonters? - VTDiggerAll reactions:814 comments2 sharesLikeCommentMost ...

comments.author: Janet Fitzpatrick
comments.text: Allow people over 30 to buy Blue Cross Blue Shield catastrophic plans! Those plans are much more affordable.

comments.author: Kristin Bryn Reid
comments.text: The health insurance system in Vermont is voing exactly as designed for the last 20 or so years We tried to warn you

comments.author: Dylan Greer
comments.text: Or hear me out… we pay a boat load of money to insurance companies… they can pay for our healthcare

comments.author: Janice Wright
comments.text: Cheaper plan = lower monthly payments, but higher deductibles, higher co-pays, less coverage, and fewer in-network doctors

comments.author: Jay Files
comments.text: My work just switched to MVP gold great insurance we had blue cross it covers almost nothing worthless insurance you have to fight with them for everything

comments.author: Amy Beth
comments.text: "higher" deductible plan my deductible is already $9500 so unless they are significantly dropping the monthly premium which is between $809-900 a month for one person this isn't helpful. Health insurance for those of us who have to purchase it from the marketplace is ridiculous.

comments.author: Ruth Miller
comments.text: I dunno...I just got a class action suit settlement payment from BCBS. I'm done with them, especially after leaving me with no gap coverage after they pulled Blue Advantage out of VT. They seem to lose money on a continuing basis.

comments.author: Matthew Funk
comments.text: It’s almost like y’all already Fcked it up
